description: For an evening or several days, a village may set ordinary work aside for shared celebration. The gathering or occasion is a 'holia' (festival). 'pharuki' (celebrate) is the deliberate activity, and a festival may contain a 'thorea' (ceremony) without becoming one by definition.
articulatory_notes: Begin with unvoiced breath through an open glottis for 'h' and round the lips into middle-height back 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and hold stressed high-front 'i'. Lower the tongue and jaw into a separate final 'a' across the 'i.a' hiatus.
usage_notes: A festival can be specified by its people, season, harvest, art, or remembered event. Its scale, inheritance, sacred standing, and the participants' feelings can be described separately.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A festival can make shared repair, harvest, art, or renewed habitat visible as communal work worth celebrating. The achievement and its participants remain available to name.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Seasonal festivals place celebration within local cycles of work and return. 'holia' supplies the general occasion while each community keeps its particular calendar and form.

semantic_domains.community: a gathering or occasion organised for shared celebration
semantic_domains.emotion: an outward setting for collective or personal joy
semantic_domains.ritual: a festive setting that can include meaningful observances
semantic_domains.temporal: a bounded occasion often tied to a recurring event or season
